title: Abdominal pain in a 22-year-old male
presentation: A 22-year-old male presents with 18 hours of periumbilical pain that has migrated to the right lower quadrant. He has had anorexia, one episode of vomiting, and a low-grade fever. Vitals: BP 122/78, HR 98, RR 16, Temp 38.2C, SpO2 99%. Exam: RLQ tenderness at McBurney's point, positive Rovsing's sign, guarding but no rigidity.

gold_standard_workup.key_decision_point: Alvarado score: migration + anorexia + RLQ tenderness + rebound + elevated WBC = high probability appendicitis, surgical consult
